title: Midjourney surprises with a full-body AI scanner without radiation
summary: The image generation company takes a leap into medicine with a device that scans the body in 60 seconds.

title: Chinese model GLM-5.2 competes with the best AI systems in the world
summary: With a 1 million token context and leading coding scores, Z.ai positions its model as a strong rival to GPT-5.5 and Fable 5.

title: GTA 6 reveals its official cover and sets pre-order date for June 25
summary: Rockstar Games announces that pre-orders for its highly anticipated title will begin in late June, raising expectations for the launch.

title: Valve releases major updates for SteamOS with significant performance improvements
summary: Versions 3.8 and 3.8.10 introduce a new kernel, support for Steam Machines, VRR optimizations, and latency reduction.

title: Anthropic adds Artifacts to Claude Code to share live web pages
summary: The new feature allows development teams to share and review web applications created during coding sessions.

title: US government accelerates electrical connections for AI data centers
summary: Federal regulators back the Trump administration's plan to prioritize grid access for artificial intelligence data centers.

title: A website called 'In the Weights' reveals whether AI models know your identity
summary: The tool analyzes AI systems' knowledge about real people, sparking debate on privacy in training data.

title: Google and the Chinese government boost the local chip and memory industry
summary: Google evaluates buying chips from CXMT while the Chinese government redirects production toward consumption and startup Enflame emerges as a new benchmark.

title: Amazon wants to compete directly with Nvidia by selling its own AI chips
summary: The company seeks to reduce its dependence on Nvidia chips by offering its custom alternatives to other customers in the cloud market.

title: OpenAI hires top executives to prepare for its expected IPO
summary: The company strengthens its executive team with key hires ahead of a possible Initial Public Offering (IPO).

title: Rivian owners sue the company over false promises about autonomous driving
summary: The class action lawsuit alleges that the company exaggerated the capabilities of its driver assistance system, creating unrealistic expectations.

title: Cyberattack on Texas government exposes 3 million driver's licenses and passports
summary: Hackers accessed a state database, stealing sensitive information in one of the largest government security breaches.

title: Yann LeCun warns that AI startups like OpenAI face a 'big bubble'
summary: Meta's chief AI scientist forecasts a market correction if promises of artificial intelligence do not translate into real value.

title: Google DeepMind treats its AI agents like rogue employees with office keys
summary: The company implements security systems that treat autonomous assistants as potential internal attack vectors.

title: OpenAI claims ChatGPT's new health mode surpasses doctor responses
summary: Internal tests show the chatbot provides more accurate and useful clinical information than that written by human doctors.

title: OpenAI researchers make AI safer and more resistant to manipulation
summary: Small doses of training in beneficial traits achieve more robust models less prone to malicious uses.

title: Meta's CTO admits employee morale is 'near its worst point'
summary: Andrew Bosworth internally acknowledges the negative impact of mass layoffs on the company's work environment.

title: Meta's AI workers rebel against the company's internal policies
summary: Discontent grows among AI employees, adding to tensions over corporate culture and technological leadership.

title: Apple plans a full glass screen without edges or bezels for future iPhones
summary: Rumors suggest that iPhone 20 and Ultra models will eliminate any frame, offering a fully immersive and touch-friendly design.

title: Huawei works on the innovative first 'vertical tri-fold smartphone' on the market
summary: The Chinese manufacturer would beat the competition with a three-section foldable design that offers an even larger screen when unfolded.
